title: Engine 1: Motion Control — Deals Die in Hesitation
summary: Pipeline velocity engine. Deals don't die from bad scripts — they die from decision latency. Motion Control is the discipline that produces typed next-moves (yes/no/not-yet/named-objection) rather than vague momentum. Twin of typed abstention: abstention refuses to commit when the band is empty; motion control refuses to stall when the band is loaded. One-liner: 'The deal died in the hesitation, not in the objection.'
